content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from autonomous agents can be reframed as a dynamic pruning and prioritization system for state-of-the-art active learning agent memory, where intrinsic curiosity signal acts as a built-in filter that automatically retains high-information unexplored patterns and discards redundant low-value memories without manual annotation. This creates a self-sustaining active learning loop that improves both memory efficiency and exploration performance as the agent scales.
